text:
Once upon a time in the quaint village of Elderglen, there lived a peculiar old owl named Oliver. He wasn't like the other owls you'd hear about; Oliver didn't hoot frequently—it was just his way of talking these days. He had a habit of collecting old posters and trading photographs of vintage planes. His favorite? A fighter jet from World War II.

One chilly autumn afternoon, while Oliver was perched atop an ancient oak mulling over his latest collection, he noticed something peculiar near his favorite perch—an old, weathered bird feather. It glowed faintly under the sun's last rays. Curiosity getting the better of him, Oliver plucked it. To his astonishment, it illuminated his entire head when he held it up to the moonlight.

Feeling oddly inspired, he decided to create a photobomb for the townsfolk. He swooped down toward the camera obscura in the village square, feasting on a mid-flight caterpillar.
